content.problemStatement:
Archi is a small boy from Uzhlyandia. For Chef's birthday, he prepared a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). Each edge in this tree has a certain length.

There are MMM speedsters on the tree, numbered 111 through MMM. For each valid iii, speedster number iii starts at time tit_iti​ from vertex number uiu_iui​ and runs straight to vertex number viv_ivi​ with constant velocity sis_isi​. Before time tit_iti​, the speedster is not considered to be present in the tree.

Chef is interested in the following question: for each vertex, what is the smallest time when a speedster is present at this vertex?
constraints: The graph described on the input is a tree
The sum of N for all test cases does not exceed 2 * 10^5
The sum of M for all test cases does not exceed 2 * 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. Each of the next N-1 lines contains three space-separated integers u, v and w denoting an edge between vertices u and v with length w. The next line contains a single integer M. For each valid i, the i-th of the next M lines contains four space-separated integers ui, vi, ti and si describing the i-th speedster.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print N lines. For each valid i, the i-th of these lines should contain a single real number — the minimum time when the i-th vertex is visited by a speedster, or -1 if it is never visited.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a modified version of Dijkstra's algorithm. We will maintain a priority queue of vertices to be processed, where the priority of each vertex is the minimum time when it can be visited by a speedster. We will also maintain an array to store the minimum time when each vertex is visited. For each speedster, we will calculate the time when it reaches each vertex and update the minimum time if necessary.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N + M) * logN) — where T is the number of test cases, N is the number of vertices and M is the number of speedsters. The reason is that we are using a priority queue to process the vertices, and each insertion and deletion operation takes O(logN) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of vertices. The reason is that we need to store the minimum time when each vertex is visited for each test case.
